what_it_does: Renders a design-system preview card for voice and tone guidance, showing use and avoid copy examples in a two-column grid.
observations: The document includes dsCard metadata for the Brand group, named "Voice & tone — use / avoid", with a 700x280 viewport.
It links to _card.css and defines file-local styles for the card, example grid, example panels, tags, heading, lead text, and accent text.
The visible guidance states that copy should be precise, second-person, and sentence-case.
The lead copy says to use direct "you" and avoid marketing voice, exclamation marks, and emoji in copy.
Two Use examples and two Avoid examples are shown, with status-colored tags and one highlighted URL preview.
standouts: The file presents brand voice rules through direct positive and negative examples.
The guidance is compact and tied to visible copy specimens rather than abstract prose.
The URL preview example uses an accent color to highlight the variable destination.
conventions: Uses CSS custom properties for colors, typography, radii, and shadows.
Uses sentence-case visible copy except where an Avoid example demonstrates all caps.
Uses semantic visual grouping through classes such as do, dont, tag, accent, pair, and ex.
Keeps all page-specific styling inline in the HTML document.
concerns: The two-column grid has no file-local responsive fallback.
The card uses a fixed min-height aligned to the declared preview viewport.
notes: This file is a compact brand voice preview card showing acceptable and unacceptable copy patterns.
questions: Should the two-column example layout adapt for narrower render targets, or is the declared viewport the only intended context?
Are the URL pattern and domain shown here placeholders or fixed product copy?
Should the Avoid examples remain visible as literal copy specimens in all generated previews?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated dsCard purpose matches the rendered content: it shows voice and tone guidance with use and avoid examples.
intention_signals: It seems intended to make brand voice rules easy to inspect by pairing concise principles with concrete examples of accepted and avoided wording.
novelty: The file combines a compact tone specification with side-by-side positive and negative copy specimens inside a visual design-system card.
contribution: Provides an example-driven brand voice artifact that a fused system would keep as a reference for copy tone, phrasing constraints, and visual presentation of use versus avoid guidance.
summary_for_embedding: A standalone HTML preview card for ConceptV brand voice guidance, showing precise second-person sentence-case copy rules with Use and Avoid examples.
intention_for_embedding: This was for documenting and visually previewing voice and tone standards through concrete copy examples in the design system.
